title: Introduction to Backlinking
content_md:
# Introduction to Backlinking

- Code: 13ed811b
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=13ed811bf5d844d99912d8bc2c9b7988
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/f3b5e2b6a05e4c04bbfb36691a014ab1?sid=32592a70-a97d-44f5-bb09-83fb06773567?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 6m 8s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: So I mentioned this in passing a couple of times, but let's dive in to make sure on the same page for what a backlink is
So alphabet, which is Google's parent company. They are now a trillion dollar company because of backlinks
So the founders Sergey Brin and Larry Page
They laid out the importance of backlinks in their Stanford PhD dissertation
This dissertation actually lays out the foundation of Google search algorithm
You can go read it online if you're super bored and need help falling to sleep or something
But what backlinks do why they're so important why they
revolutionize this space is
They solved the fundamental problem that every search engine struggled with before Google
That is how do we determine if a website is trustworthy?
How do we decide if a website is trustworthy enough to send my users to that website?
Right. Yeah, who did this by manually reviewing websites and giving us a curated list?
Alta Vista just basically didn't do this
They did a terrible job of it and Google did it by using backlinks as a signal of trust
Now the way that this rank position works. It's basically if you're significantly below the first page then Google can't use
User engagement metrics
So your rank position is largely going to be based on backlinks
It's going to be based on on-page content and then there's sort of a multiplying factor of your overall site authority
Now if you are on the first page in other words if enough people are interacting with your content
Google will instead use user experience now interacting with your content means on Chrome on Android on any of the platforms
Like Google owns Google did not build Chrome and Android out of the goodness of their hearts
They built them to feed more data into its algorithm to better determine
trustworthiness and goal completion one big example of this they often talk about is
Mist call rates for a local business if somebody tries to call your business and you don't answer that is a bad user experience
Google is tracking that that's one of the reasons that they've released Android
So they could see how people are interacting with local businesses and improve their local search algorithm
So in other words answer the phone. It's good for ranking. Also, you can't get leads if you don't answer the phone
This is a study that was done. It's was done a little while ago, but it's still
Demonstrates the power of backlinks. So here we have the total number of referring domains coming in
Against the Google position so we can see that it's a pretty strong correlation
As the Google position drops, so does the number of referring domains just demonstrating again that backlinks are very powerful for ranking
Google says they aren't Google is lying backlinks are very powerful for ranking. In fact
We know that backlinks strength represents around 50% of rank position
That is based on a correlation of a h refs backlink data
They're the best third-party tool for tracking backlinks and if you correlate their backlink data with Google rank position the correlations about 50%
So we don't know maybe a h refs miss some backlinks and the correlations actually higher
But regardless backlinks are hugely important to rank. That's also true of GBPs, right?
Now the number of referring domains is the important factor
Multiple links from the same domain eventually loses its importance
So right don't sponsor the same softball team every single year
You're going to want to find different teams with different organizations different websites to provide that referring domain to diversity
Purely from an SEO perspective if there are other reasons to support the same team over and over again
Then go for it, of course
And the quality is better than the quantity. This is why we basically say
We usually only need a handful of these high trust-building backlinks these super powerful local ones
And when we look at the backlinks that are purely designed to get content indexed and crawled
We really just need one, right? So we don't need to go crazy
You'll often hear me talk about a PBN the reason for this when we're building relevance, right?
And building relevance means writing content when we're writing this content to build relevance
We will generally seek out one link per article
Okay, we don't need to go crazy
We don't need more than that and I'm typically looking for the least expensive link that I can find
That's a high enough quality to get Google to crawl it to index it to trust that it isn't just slop
So the that typically means that we're using PBMs
It stands for private blog network
Basically, it's a network of sites that you own and control but you've hidden the fact from Google that you own and control these
So Google still thinks they're independent, right now
From 2017, this is obviously very outdated. So my guess is this number is even higher
All the websites on the internet 80% of the websites on the internet basically exist to get
Link authority to the 20% that are generating traffic, okay?
So, you know PBN this fascination the requirement for backlinks to drive rank position has
Exploded in such a way that now most of the content on the internet only exists to rank other content
Okay, I
Wouldn't recommend that you try to build your own PBN it can be done
And we will have a lesson on how to do that
but the challenge is if
If you don't hide the footprint correctly then your entire network is going to get d-indexed
This won't hurt the rank position of the sites that I was pointing to I have not seen it happen where a
PBN was discovered and it actually hurt the site I was linked to this is a common misconception
I hear this all the time what happens instead if you're using a PBN to get into a higher rank position
Then that PBN got indexed you'll likely lose rank position back to where you were if you hadn't got any of the PBN links
So it basically erases the PBN building authority getting individual pages
indexed getting individual pages trusted
And that all goes away. It doesn't hurt you
But it brings you back to where you would be if you hadn't used any PBNs
Hopefully this explanation of back links has been helpful

summary: Backlinks are crucial for determining a website's trustworthiness and ranking on Google. They solve the problem of identifying trustworthy sites by using links as signals, with correlations showing that backlinks significantly impact rank position.
key_points: Google uses backlinks to rank websites
Correlation between backlinks and rank position is strong
